symptoms: I had a dog bite and the area now tingles, with fever and confusion. Trying to drink water causes spasms and I feel weakness spreading.
A bat scratched me, and now I have numbness at the wound, headache, and fear of water. I am drooling and my legs feel partly paralyzed.
Weeks after an animal bite, I got fever, anxiety, and burning at the old bite site. I cannot swallow liquids and I am becoming delirious.
The bite on my hand tingles and burns, and now I have headache, agitation, and hydrophobia. My arms are getting weak.
After a stray dog bite, I feel pins-and-needles at the wound, fever, and confusion. Water makes me choke and panic.
A bat bite healed, but now the spot hurts and tingles again. I have fever, hallucinations, and paralysis after throat spasms with water.
I have fever and itching at a recent bite site, then confusion and trouble swallowing. Seeing water terrifies me and I am drooling.
The old bite wound became numb and painful, and now I am restless, hydrophobic, and weak in my limbs.
After being bitten by an animal, I got headache, fever, and tingling at the wound. Drinking water triggers choking and I feel progressive paralysis.
I was bitten by a dog and now have burning at the bite site, confusion, excessive saliva, and fear of water.
